6.reason: [EOF is an integer constant (typically -1) and cannot be appended to a std::string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect content.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [EOF is an integer constant (typically -1) and cannot be appended to a std::string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect content.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [EOF is an integer constant (typically -1), not a char. Appending it to a std::string may cause issues because std::string treats it as a character with value 255 (if char is unsigned) or cause undefined behavior. The correct way to mark end-of-file is to not append anything and check index against content.length().]

309.reason: [Same issue: EOF is an integer constant and cannot be appended to a std::string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect content.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: EOF is an integer constant and cannot be appended to a std::string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect content.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as in Lexer.cpp. Appending EOF to a std::string is incorrect and can lead to undefined behavior. The content should be read without appending EOF.]
